methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Claros, a seed-stage climate and energy company that has raised $30M, is valued through triangulation against a peer set of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies alongside 1 public comparable (LNZA). The broader frontier-tech climate and energy universe encompasses 9 private and 1 public comparable, including peers such as TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape.

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression method, using total raised as a proxy for current value, applied a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement, implying $75M. The peer-median method examined the four climate and energy peers at Series A stage—Watershed ($1.8B), Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M)—yielding a median valuation of $175M, with a range of $110M to $1.8B reflecting material dispersion in the peer set.

Triangulation of these two methods produces a valuation range of $64M (low) to $201M (high), with a median of $125M. The wide dispersion between stage-progression ($75M) and peer-median ($175M) reflects genuine uncertainty: the peer-median is pulled upward by heterogeneous Series A outcomes, while the stage-progression method relies on a standard multiplier that may not capture Claros' specific position or market conditions. Key uncertainties include whether Claros' differentiation supports a valuation above the peer floor ($110M) and the degree to which later-stage peer outcomes are predictive of seed-stage value creation.
